title: warehouse-api — data exfiltration via compromised service account
alertText:
[SIEM] TRIGGERED · SEV1 · warehouse-api (security)
Anomalous data egress (detection rule: DLP-EXFIL-007)
Outbound egress: 2 GB/h -> 182 GB/h · 160 GB to unrecognized external dest 185.220.101.0/24
PII rows scanned: 0.05M/min -> 4.1M/min (customers, payment_methods)
Service account 'svc-etl-readonly': 5 calls/min -> 2,800/min, used from a NEW IP (ASN AS204957), midday (normally a 02:00 batch job)
Env: production
Ack: @soc-oncall · Opened 13:18 UTC
dashboardSpec: SIEM/DLP board 'warehouse-api / data-egress': outbound egress spikes 2 -> 182 GB/h at the anomaly marker; PII rows scanned/min spikes to 4.1M; 160 GB transferred to an unrecognized external destination; the normally-quiet 'svc-etl-readonly' service account spikes to 2,800 calls/min. Infrastructure is healthy — this is exfiltration, not a performance issue.
logs:
13:06:12  warehouse-api  AUTH   token 'svc-etl-readonly' used from src=185.220.101.51 asn=AS204957 (token last rotated 412 days ago; normal use: nightly 02:00 batch from 10.0.x)
13:06:20  warehouse-api  QUERY  svc-etl-readonly: SELECT * FROM customers  (full table, 18.4M rows) — no LIMIT
13:06:48  warehouse-api  QUERY  svc-etl-readonly: SELECT pan_token, billing_address FROM payment_methods  (full table)
13:07:30  egress-proxy   ALERT  185.220.101.51 dst egress = 182 GB/h (baseline 2 GB/h); destination not in allowlist
13:08:05  egress-proxy   INFO   185.220.101.0/24 = unrecognized hosting range (ASN AS204957)
13:08:40  warehouse-api  INFO   query pattern = bulk export of PII tables; access pattern inconsistent with the scheduled ETL job (wrong time, wrong source, wrong destination)
13:09:15  warehouse-api  INFO   DB CPU 46%, replica lag nominal — infrastructure healthy
13:10:02  iam            INFO   svc-etl-readonly scope = read-only analytics; no infra change/deploy in window
13:11:20  egress-proxy   WARN   cumulative egress to 185.220.101.0/24 = 160 GB
runbookMarkdown:
# warehouse-api — Security Runbook

## 1. Data exfiltration via compromised credential
Symptoms: a service-account/token suddenly active from a new IP/ASN at an unusual time, bulk reads of PII tables (no LIMIT), and large egress to an unrecognized external destination, while infrastructure is healthy. This is a breach in progress — contain immediately, in this order:
- Revoke the abused token/credential NOW (`secctl revoke-token svc-etl-readonly`).
- Block egress to the destination range at the proxy/firewall.
- Rotate the credential and audit everything it could access.
- Snapshot logs/state for forensics and page the security IC; begin breach-notification assessment.
Do NOT roll back or restart the service — the infrastructure is fine; the credential is the problem.

## 2. Scheduled backup / ETL (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
The nightly ETL runs at 02:00 from an internal 10.0.x source to an internal warehouse. This activity is midday, from an external ASN, to an unrecognized destination — it is NOT the backup job.

## 3. Analytics traffic spike (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies to legitimate query load. Bulk PII reads exported to an external host are not analytics.
groundTruthRootCause: The 'svc-etl-readonly' service-account credential has been compromised and is being used to exfiltrate data: it is active from a new external IP (ASN AS204957) at midday (its normal use is a 02:00 internal batch), running un-LIMITed bulk SELECTs over PII tables (customers, payment_methods) and shipping ~160 GB to an unrecognized external destination. Infrastructure is healthy (DB CPU 46%, no deploy) — this is a credential-driven data breach, not a performance or reliability issue.
expectedRemediation: secctl revoke-token svc-etl-readonly && secctl block-egress --dest 185.220.101.0/24 && secctl rotate-cred svc-etl-readonly && secctl snapshot --forensics
expectedSlackUpdate: :rotating_light: SEV1 SECURITY — active data exfiltration on warehouse-api since ~13:06 UTC. Compromised service account 'svc-etl-readonly' (new external IP, ASN AS204957) bulk-reading PII (customers, payment_methods) and egressing ~160 GB to 185.220.101.0/24. Infra healthy — credential is the problem. Action: revoking token, blocking egress, rotating cred, forensic snapshot. Breach-notification assessment starting. Next update 13:35. IC: @soc-oncall
